creator: artur.zabronski@gmail.com
text:
The problem is connected with the notebook HP NX6125 and occurs only when kernel is booted with hw.psm.synaptics_support=1.

When the touchpad is not used for a few seconds, attempt to move the cursor fails because it is frozen, after few seconds (4-5) it starts working and keeps doing it but only when it is constantly used (with 1-2 seconds' breaks in using it). After some time with no move, the problem appears again.

When touchpad is not used in kernel prints "psm0: lost interrupt?" with 1-2 seconds interval.

When hw.psm.synaptics_support=0 (touchpad working as normal mouse) problem not occurs but kernel still prints "psm0: lost interrupt?". 

dmesg (with hw.psm.synaptics_support=1):

	The Regents of the University of California. All rights reserved.
FreeBSD is a registered trademark of The FreeBSD Foundation.

How-To-Repeat: Set hw.psm.synaptics_support=1 in /boot/loader.conf, boot kernel and try using /dev/psm0 device e.g. in X (driver synaptics/mouse doesn't care - freezes occuring in both).
